ops.title: capt-hook `--hooks test` loads the REGISTERED pack from cache not the tree; @latest=release tag; mcp_active-gated pack messages make inline patterns mode-dependent

ops.body:
Hard-won mechanics from cc-notes task 4e0b9ac (the "5 codified rows fail on warn-message drift" pack fix).

**RESOLVED 2026-07-14 — task 4e0b9ac + sibling ebf5434 both closed done.** The concurrent plugin/hooks rework fixed the 5 drift rows in cc-notes v0.30.1 (`cdf40e1d`, "pure mcp_active + deterministic record inline tests"), on main + released as v0.31.0 (`de470cd3`). The mode-agnostic fragments that actually SHIPPED differ from the handoff spec below: record.py cp/mv/Write rows → `Warn(pattern="Record a cc-notes log entry")`, ephemeral → `Warn(pattern="purge-bound path")`, plan → `Warn(pattern="Native TaskCreate/TaskUpdate is your private")`. Verified all nudge rows PASS in an MCP-active shell (573/573 cc-notes, 868/868 cc-context) and CI green (CLI-mode) — mode-agnostic approach confirmed in both modes. ebf5434 ("import of touched_comment_blocks") was never a real upstream gap: the symbol shipped in capt-hook 9.10.0; the local "1 import errors" was a stale `uv tool install capt-hook` (v9.2.0), fixed by `uv tool upgrade` → see [[capt-hook-local-testing]]. Below is the pre-resolution investigation, kept for the mechanics.

- **`uvx capt-hook --hooks <dir> test` tests the REGISTERED pack from cache, not the working tree.** When a pack is registered in `.claude/hooks/packs.toml` (e.g. cc-notes → `github:yasyf/cc-notes@latest`), capt-hook loads its hooks by name from the content-addressed cache `~/.cache/captain-hook/packs/<name>@<sha>`, resolved from its source — even inside the pack's OWN repo. Editing `plugin/hooks/record.py` and re-running the test shows the OLD cached pack. Working-tree edits only take effect after commit+push+re-resolve. The cc-notes README says to verify with `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test`, but that's the cache too. To get a real pre-push signal, copy the hooks dir to /tmp and RENAME the pack in `capt-hook.toml` (`name = "…-verify"`) so it doesn't shadow the cached one — but note /tmp is non-MCP (see below).

- **packs.toml `@latest` = newest RELEASE TAG, not the branch tip.** cc-notes pin cached as `latest@9e8fb84` = tag v0.27.0; newest was v0.28.0. `pack update <name>` re-resolves @latest to the newest release. Landing a pack fix in a consumer (cc-context 312/312) needs a NEW release of the pack repo, not just a pushed commit to main.

- **cc-notes pack warn messages are gated on `mcp_active(evt)` (plugin/hooks/common.py:270).** MCP-form ("log_add tool", "attach param", "task_add tool") when a cc-notes MCP liveness marker sits under the repo's git dir; CLI-form ("--attach", "cc-notes log add", "cc-notes task add") otherwise. CI's `hook-tests` job runs non-MCP (no marker) → CLI-form; a dev shell with the MCP running → MCP-form. An inline `tests={Input: Warn(pattern=…)}` row has ONE static pattern, so a mode-specific pattern passes in one env and fails the other — this is why the "drift" shows only in a dev shell yet CI is green, and why the naive MCP-form fix would BREAK CI. Fix pack-message drift with a MODE-AGNOSTIC fragment common to both branches (precedent: `record.py:632` already uses `Warn(pattern="attach")`).

- Editing files in cc-notes' shared jj working copy `@` while a concurrent session works there got my edit auto-snapshotted into their commit and swept away by them ("fix(hooks): drop record.py test-expectation WIP swept into the rewire commit"). Isolate cross-session pack edits with `jj workspace add -r main` — see [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]].

- **The cache trap bites cc-context's OWN ccx pack too, and only CI catches the miss (2026-07-13, grep-guard review-fix).** cc-context's ccx pack is attach-only (`pack attach ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks` at SessionStart), NOT in packs.toml. Plain `uvx capt-hook test` from the repo evaluates a cached/attached ccx pack — a working-tree edit to `grep_guards.py` that tightened the guard did NOT show up, so the local run was falsely clean while CI went red. CI runs `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test` (ci.yml:147) in a FRESH runner: no prior cache, so it content-builds the ccx pack from the checked-out tree and tests the real edit. Locally, run that same `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test` to reproduce CI (a warm local cache may still be keyed by content and pick up the change — it did here, 733 tests — but plain `uvx capt-hook test` did NOT). Second gotcha that turns this into a cross-pack surprise: capt-hook `test` evaluates each pack's inline `Input` against ALL registered hooks, so another pack's benign-neighbor `Allow` row (`handler:Input(command='grep -rn foo /etc/hosts')`) FAILS when a stricter ccx grep-guard edit newly blocks that command — the failing test name belongs to the other pack, but the fix is in the ccx guard. Lesson: a guard-tightening isn't verified until `uvx capt-hook --hooks plugin/hooks test` is green, and pytest (`474 passed`) does NOT cover inline `tests={}` rows.

- **grep-guard invariant refresher (same review):** `-c`/`-q`/`-l`/`-L` output is one line per operand ONLY without recursion; under `-r`/`-R` a count fans out one line per file in the tree, so the output-bounded size-cap skip must gate on `not recursive`. Forfeiting the stat lane for ALL recursion over-blocks (`grep -rn foo <small file>` doesn't flood — `-r` on a regular file greps just that file). BRE→Rust interval translation must reject a leading or stacked interval (`\{2\}`, `a\+\{2\}`) — an interval is a quantifier — and honor GNU's 32767 `RE_DUP_MAX` ceiling (above it GNU errors, Rust compiles → a wrong rewrite).

Related: [[capt-hook-cc-context-wiring]], [[capt-hook-local-testing]], [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]].
